phase-scope.phase-29: Deterministic MTProto provisioning core implemented and verified locally.
phase-scope.phase-34: Stabilization gate must prove verified users can receive one free indefinite owner proxy while unverified users and stale rotation states remain safe.
phase-scope.phase-38: Existing SNI, base secret, salt, and tg link stability must survive the runtime target move from RU-only to DE-backed edge.
phase-scope.phase-41: Provisioning must return KPprotoN fake-TLS ee owner payloads and explicitly reissue official dd credential-mode assignments.

V-M-043.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py -v

verification-levels.module-local: Pure deterministic SNI, secret derivation, tg://proxy assembly, validation, and idempotency tests use fixed vectors and fake repository/session adapters.
verification-levels.wave-level: Runs with V-M-042 so issue/reissue persists only restore-safe assignment data and can rebuild owner-safe payloads.
verification-levels.phase-level: Phase-29 cannot pass if unverified users can get a proxy, raw secrets are logged, or fake-TLS vectors are only shape-tested without golden assertions.

module-checks.check-1: SNI generation is stable and wildcard-safe for krotpn.xyz.
module-checks.check-2: Derived fake-TLS credential differs from raw base secret and matches golden vectors.
module-checks.check-3: Repeated issuance is idempotent and returns the same canonical assignment.
module-checks.check-4: Missing, blank, malformed, or rotated base secret/salt returns explicit reissue-required or config-error states without partial persistence or backend startup crash.
module-checks.check-5: The generated tg://proxy payload is returned only to the owner-safe service response and is not persisted or logged raw.
module-checks.check-6: Pending email registration and unverified users cannot trigger MTProto assignment issuance.
module-checks.check-7: Verified-email activation plus owner dashboard access produces one idempotent MTProto assignment and owner-only tg://proxy response.
module-checks.check-8: Changing runtime policy target to DE does not change SNI generation, fake-TLS derivation, proxy port, or owner tg://proxy payload for existing assignments.
module-checks.check-9: New owner payloads use server=u-*.krotpn.xyz and secret=ee...; official dd payloads are not treated as active production links.

phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-1: Free MTProto assignment is indefinite by KrotPN subscription/trial lifetime and does not expire with the three-day KrotPN VPN trial.
phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-2: Base secret and salt remain test-only in automated gates; production secrets are never required or printed during Phase-34 local verification.

scenarios.scenario-1: Verified user receives a personal tg://proxy link with derived per-SNI credential.
scenarios.scenario-2: Reopening the account dashboard rebuilds the same owner-safe payload from assignment state.
scenarios.scenario-3: Explicit reissue after rotation creates a new assignment state or rotation marker according to M-042 policy.
scenarios.scenario-4: Missing or blank base secret, missing or blank salt, or invalid secret format blocks issuance safely.
scenarios.scenario-5: Unsafe SNI characters, wrong domain, duplicate SNI collision, or invalid port fail deterministically.
scenarios.scenario-6: Unverified, inactive, or missing users do not receive assignment rows or tg://proxy payloads.
scenarios.scenario-7: Existing assignment payload is rebuilt unchanged while the runtime bridge applies policy to the DE edge.
scenarios.scenario-8: Phase-41 reissue converts a superseded official assignment into a KPprotoN fake-TLS owner payload without leaking raw secrets.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Never log full tg://proxy links, derived secret, base secret, or salt.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Logs may include user id, assignment id, masked SNI, credential mode, and rotation marker only.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-29 M-043 can be executed autonomously with fake repository adapters and local DB tests; no live Telegram connectivity, DNS mutation, runtime edge changes, or deploy/install edits are allowed.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: V-M-043 target tests passed locally inside the combined Phase-29 pytest run.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: Blank MTProto env values regression test added after clean VPS deploy exposed backend startup failure from empty secrets.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 verification requires no user proxy link reissue when moving policy apply to the DE-backed runtime.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design restores fake-TLS ee payload assertions and supersedes official dd payloads for production.
